title: salvia pratensis var. rostrata
intro: Salvia pratensis var. rostrata, also known as Red Salvia or Longhorned Sage, is a perennial plant species within the Salvia genus, characterized by its reddish-purple flowers and distinctive, elongated inflorescences. The medicinal properties of Salvia pratensis var. rostrata include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its capacity to reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Salvia pratensis var. rostrata often involve infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
